Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Overlord (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== House of Blades ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 2''' — Leah intends to speak with the Overlord regarding certain matters.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 4''' — The Overlord is a figure who holds prisoners of war.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 5''' — The Overlord is a power that villagers cannot stand up to.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 11''' — Overlords are a class of powerful beings mentioned by the children.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 16''' — The Overlord has received requests for parley.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 16''' — The Overlord has a Master of Household.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 17''' — The Overlord is a figure the Myrians are standing against.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 17''' — The Overlord personally took Leah.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 22''' — Simon killed an Overlord.}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 2''' — The Overlord is dead.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 9''' — Each Overlord guards one Hanging Tree.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 16''' — The Overlord is in an alliance with the Grandmasters and his Travelers.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 18''' — An Overlord is a title for someone serving the throne of Damasca.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
